Initializes the new timer service.

# Purpose
This is a C header file that declares a function prototype for [`new_timer_service_init`](<#new_timer_service_init>). The function is intended to initialize a new timer service. The use of `#pragma once` ensures that the file is included only once in a single compilation, preventing duplicate definitions. This file is part of a larger codebase and provides an interface for initializing timer-related functionality.
# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### new\_timer\_service\_init<!-- {{#callable_declaration:new_timer_service_init}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../src/fw/services/common/new_timer/new_timer_service.h#L19>)

Initializes the new timer service.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up the new timer service before any timer-related operations. It prepares necessary resources and configurations, such as creating semaphores and task queues, and initializing task parameters. Call this function once during the system startup to ensure that the timer service is ready for use. It must be called before any other functions that depend on the timer service.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`new_timer_service_init`](<new_timer.c.md#new_timer_service_init>)  (Implementation)
